title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the Clout family inheritance with Inspector Spence, noting that because Robert André Hercule died in Africa, Rosaline Clout serves as the barrier to the family inheriting Gordon's money. Poirot suspects Rosaline, observing that despite her unstable mental state, she is physically strong and has a long life ahead of her to covet the inheritance.

After leaving the police station, Poirot is accosted by Aunt Caroline, who laments the materialistic life of the late Colonel Porter. During a clumsy encounter in which she drops a codfish and a can of golden syrup, she mentions seeing a familiar face of someone deceased while seeking change for a phone call. Nearby, Mrs. Leonine Clout joins a line at the Peacock Candy Store.

Poirot walks toward White House, reflecting on the path Charles Trenton took before his death, where he encountered Rosaline Clout. At White House, Poirot finds Lynn Marchmont resting under an apple tree. Lynn expresses her desire for David Hunter to be found innocent, suggesting the police are prejudiced against him; Poirot clarifies that it is the jury, rather than the police, who hold prejudice. He reveals he has been gathering information through gossip, including the movements of a man named Jato and the unusual happiness Rosaline Clout displayed while at a farm with Rolly Clout. Lynn admits her family has all attempted to get money from Rosaline and confesses she is uncertain about her potential marriage to Rolly Crouch, a decision complicated by David Hunter's sudden appearance in the woods.
